content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN. You can perform the following operation on it any number of times (possibly, zero):


Choose any two distinct indices iii and jjj (1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N1\leq i , j \leq N1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N and i‚Č†ji \neq jiÓÄ†=j), and multiply both AiA_iAi‚Äč and AjA_jAj‚Äč by ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.

That is, set AiA_iAi‚Äč to ‚ąíAi-A_i‚ąíAi‚Äč, and also AjA_jAj‚Äč to ‚ąíAj-A_j‚ąíAj‚Äč.
Find the **maximum** possible sum of the array AAA after performing this operation several times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN ‚ÄĒ the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible sum of AAA after several operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4‚čÖ1054\cdot 10^54‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The sum of the array is 777, which is the best we can do. No operations are needed.
Test case 222: Consider the following operation:

Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=5j = 5j=5. The array becomes [3,4,6,2,1][3, 4, 6, 2, 1][3,4,6,2,1].
Its sum is now 161616, which is the maximum possible.
